message:
fix(logging): clamp fsqlite submodule log volume so -v/-vv stays useful

`default_filter` previously enabled fsqlite at the same level as
beads_rust at each verbosity step (`-vv` → `fsqlite=debug`,
`-vvv` → `trace`). fsqlite's internal submodules
(`fsqlite_btree`, `fsqlite_vdbe`, `fsqlite_pager`, the cx
checkpoint paths) emit at `debug` for every row and page touched,
so on a bulk import or rebuild they drowned out the
beads_rust-specific logs by many orders of magnitude — exactly
the case where `-v`/`-vv` is supposed to help diagnose.

Re-tier the fsqlite spec so each verbosity level enables the
fsqlite information you'd actually want at that level:

  -v   →  beads_rust=debug, fsqlite=warn
          (fsqlite stays at warn — the operator asked for
           beads_rust insight, not pager/btree dumps)

  -vv  →  beads_rust=debug,
          fsqlite=info,
          fsqlite_btree=warn,
          fsqlite_vdbe=warn,
          fsqlite_pager=warn
          (operator-relevant fsqlite events bubble up; per-row
           submodule chatter stays clamped)

  -vvv →  beads_rust=trace,
          fsqlite=debug,
          fsqlite_btree=info,
          fsqlite_vdbe=info
          (last-resort: full beads trace + fsqlite debug, with
           the noisiest submodules raised to info instead of
           debug — still asked for, but not utterly drowning)

Default verbosity (no flags) under debug builds also picks up the
new `fsqlite=warn` clamp.

The existing `default_filter_varies_with_verbosity` unit test is
updated to assert the new strings exactly.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust
passes. Untracked `fix_test_locks.sh` is intentionally not staged.
